text:
There once was an old man named Sam who lived alone in a quiet cabin. Every morning Sam would hear birds chirping, insects buzzing, and the wind whispering through the trees. Eventually, he made up his mind to get out of his nest.

Sam set off one day toward a distant town, intending to pick up some hardware. As he walked, he felt invigorated, as if shedding years of boredom off his bones. He passed through fields of wildflowers swaying in the breeze and rivers weaving through green corridors. He stopped by a cozy little shop where an old woman greeted him.

"Good morning!" she crooned. "What are you looking for today?"

"A new adventure," Sam said with a grin.

He bought a shovel and some bait. The next day, armed with his new tools, he struck out for woodlands. The path was difficult—boggy, overgrown—but he had determination. As he labored to clear a path, he found himself
